data.title: U.S. Treasury's AI Initiatives and Trade Agreement Developments
data.description: According to PANews, U.S. Treasury Secretary Besent announced that Paras Malik is leading the Department of the Treasury's efforts related to artificial intelligence. Besent also stated that a trade agreement has been largely finalized, with the potential for tariff revenues to exceed $300 billion by 2026.

data.description: According to PANews, U.S. Treasury Secretary Besent stated that the current situation does not represent the largest reshaping of trade patterns since 1935. Previous regulatory policies have compelled the relocation of manufacturing overseas.

data.title: Bank of England Signals Possible End to Rate Cuts Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description:
According to PANews, the Bank of England has reiterated its cautious approach to reducing borrowing costs, suggesting that its rate-cutting actions may soon conclude. The central bank noted that as interest rates decrease, the restrictive nature of monetary policy diminishes, no longer directly stating that the policy remains restrictive. The Bank of England emphasized that there is no predetermined path for interest rates.

The halt in rate cuts could be a setback for Chancellor Reeves and Prime Minister Starmer, who have been striving to fulfill promises to voters by accelerating the UK's sluggish economic growth. Governor Bailey described the decision for the fifth rate cut since last August as "very balanced," although he acknowledged that rates are still on a downward trajectory.

Among the nine policymakers, four sought to maintain borrowing costs due to concerns over high inflation. The Monetary Policy Committee faced difficulty reaching consensus, leading to its first-ever occurrence of two rate votes. The committee is divided on how to address inflation, which the Bank of England predicts will soon double its 2% target, and the worsening unemployment situation.

Governor Bailey and four colleagues supported reducing the interest rate from 4.25% to 4%. The four members advocating for unchanged rates included Deputy Governor for Monetary Policy Lombardelli, who for the first time diverged from the majority, and Chief Economist Pill, who also voted to keep the rate at 4.25%.

data.title: Whales Withdraw Significant Ethereum Amounts from Exchanges
data.description: According to BlockBeats, several large Ethereum holders have withdrawn substantial amounts of ETH from exchanges over the past 10 hours. The address starting with 0x526 withdrew 3,000 ETH, valued at approximately $11.17 million. Another address beginning with 0xF436 removed 5,630 ETH, worth around $20.91 million. Additionally, the address starting with 0x1cd withdrew 1,080 ETH, equivalent to about $4 million. An entity named Abraxas also withdrew 1,700 ETH, valued at approximately $6.45 million.

data.title: Bank of England Reduces Interest Rates by 25 Basis Points
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the Bank of England has lowered its benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points, reducing it from 4.25% to 4%. This marks the fifth rate cut in the current cycle, aligning with market expectations.

data.title: Coinbase and Indian Minister Explore Blockchain Push — What It Means for SHIB
data.description:
Summary: Why is Coinbase’s meeting with Karnataka’s IT Minister significant? The meeting spotlights increasing government interest in using blockchain technology for digital governance and public services. It shows a move from viewing blockchain as just an investment to seeing it as important infrastructure. This signals growing adoption of blockchain in real-world applications and potential collaboration opportunities. 🎧 Listen to This Article Prefer to listen? data.title: Buterin Urges Faster Ethereum Withdrawals — Could This Boost Shibarium Too?
data.description:
Summary: Why does Vitalik Buterin want Ethereum Layer 2 withdrawals to be faster? Buterin believes waiting days to withdraw from Layer 2s is too long and hurts user experience. He says quicker withdrawals would reduce reliance on insecure bridging solutions and lower the cost of capital. Faster, trustless exits would help Ethereum Layer 1 remain the core of its economic ecosystem. 🎧 Listen to This Article Prefer to listen? Hit play below to hear the narrated version
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/08/07/buterin-urges-faster-ethereum-withdrawals-could-this-boost-shibarium-too/

data.title: 256 ETH Stolen in AI Scam Hijacking YouTube Channels for Crypto Theft
data.description:
Summary: How are scammers using YouTube to steal crypto? Scammers hijack old, trusted YouTube channels to promote fake trading bots with hidden smart contracts that drain funds. Victims are tricked into deploying these contracts and sending crypto, losing thousands.   🎧 Listen to This Article Prefer to listen? Hit play below to hear the narrated version. Cybersecurity company SentinelLABS has issued a report revealing that cybercriminals are exploiting long-established, credible-seeming crypto YouTube channels to advertise a fraudulent trading bot embedded with a malicious smart contract that drains users’ funds
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/08/07/256-eth-stolen-in-ai-scam-hijacking-youtube-channels-for-crypto-theft/
